markdownRemark.excerpt: I’ve been thinking about the best place on the web to talk about some of the things I do for a while now, and have decided to just bite the bullet and build a…
markdownRemark.html:
I’ve been thinking about the best place on the web to talk about some of the things I do for a while now, and have decided to just bite the bullet and build a blog.

After a short amount of research I decided to go with Hugo, and I’m currently hosting this using Github pages (I’ll sort out a custom domain soon). I like the idea of static site generation tools combined with markdown, and Hugo seems faster than Jekyll (from the small amount of reading I did).

Some might ask “why not Medium?”

  • I’m not sure I like how public Medium is (for my content anyway).
  • I figure what I have to say isn’t that important.
  • Not everything is going to be technical/informative, some of it may end up just deeply personal, or (more than likely) rants.

Other reasons:

  • I wanted to experiment with static site creation tools, as they seem way more compelling than something like Wordpress.
  • I yearn for the days of the old web - a time before Facebook, Twitter and what I feel has been the massive de-personalisation of the web.

This will be a place to share my projects (or the ones I’m interested in making public at least) and maybe some opinions. No doubt some of the content will be old projects that I’m just getting around to documenting now.

Should be fun. Don’t be surprised if it’s abandoned in a couple of weeks.

frontmatter.title: Farmhand: A multi-function monitor for precision agriculture
